summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #267: a TreeView agnostic component in a Rust UI library (ars-ui), including the DnD reorder variant, with full spec sync, tests, and audit.
summary.outcome: TreeView agnostic core fully implemented with 85 unit tests, 26 snapshots, proptest, 99% line/region coverage, spec synced, 3-phase audit findings landed, mutation gate policy changed, and a PR opened to GitHub.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() iterates visible_indices only — it does NOT return nodes hidden under collapsed parents. ExpandAll (and any operation needing all nodes) requires new all_keys()/all_nodes() accessors.

Key does not implement Borrow<str>. The spec used String-based node identity throughout (event payloads, BTreeSet operations), which would not compile and could not round-trip Key::Int values. Entire spec required retyping to Key.
Key::int(5) serializes to '5', but Key::from("5") yields Key::String("5") — the two never compare equal in a BTreeSet<Key>. String-based identity in specs is a silent correctness pit.
selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys, but the component must seed disabled_keys from TreeItem.disabled flags in init — otherwise disabled nodes are silently selectable.
EffectName has a blanket impl; manual impls cause a compiler conflict and must be removed.
Callback<T> implements Clone + Debug + PartialEq via pointer identity. It is safe to store in Props structs.
Every sibling nav/input component implements on_props_changed for controlled-prop syncing. Omitting it means controlled selected/expanded updates and post-reorder prop re-supply are silently ignored.

DraggableCollection::selection(&self) requires live selection state, which the bare collection doesn't own — so DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls for TreeView belong in the adapter tasks (#366/#509), not the agnostic core.
Navigation integration tests use a single navigation.rs file, not per-component files. New component tests append there.
spec-compile-snippets runs syn::parse_file per block — it is a syntax check only, not full type resolution. Blocks don't need to be self-contained.
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md. Edits must target AGENTS.md directly.
.agents/ and .claude/ skill files are hardlinked (same inode); editing either updates both.
Pre-commit hooks re-stage all tracked modifications, which collapses a planned multi-commit split into a single commit.

code.finding: visible_keys() (and nodes()) sits in impl<T: CollectionItem> block and iterates visible_indices only. all_keys()/all_nodes() accessors were added to the no-bound impl<T> block instead.
selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys at this line, but only if disabled_keys was seeded during init — the component is responsible for populating it from TreeItem.disabled flags.

workflow: TDD is enforced by convention: write failing tests first, confirm red, implement, confirm green. This caught the init seeding bug for collection-default-expanded nodes before it could ship.
Snapshot tests (insta) create .new files on first run. All generated snapshots must be reviewed for correctness before accepting with INSTA_UPDATE=new.
cargo-mutants buffers progress; live results land in mutants.out/ incrementally during the run, not on stdout.
cargo xci --profile fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (runs clippy, tests, snapshots, spec-validate, compile-snippets across all relevant crates).
Background tasks can be cancelled mid-run via TaskStop when their prerequisite is removed (e.g., policy change making the mutation gate optional).
Removing a mandatory gate (mutation) should update both the workflow doc (AGENTS.md) and any skill that enforces it (post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md) to stay consistent.
Coverage analysis via llvm-cov after initial test pass efficiently identifies residual gaps; targeting uncovered lines before mutation testing is more efficient than relying on mutation output alone.

friction: The spec used String-based node identity throughout, which was incompatible with the actual codebase convention (Key) and would not have compiled — required a large-scale spec rewrite across §1–§6.
TreeCollection::nodes() returning visible-only nodes contradicted the spec's ExpandAll semantics, requiring new ars-collections accessors and a spec sync before the component could be written.
The mutation rerun was stopped due to a mid-session policy change, leaving stale background pollers that fired exit code 1 and caused confusing task notifications.
Multiple fallback ScheduleWakeup timers fired after the mutation run was stopped, requiring the assistant to explain the superseded state multiple times.
cargo-mutants does not stream per-mutant results to stdout, making interim progress checks unreliable (the poller contended on the cargo lock and exited with code 1).
The planned two-commit split (feature vs. policy) collapsed into one because the pre-commit hook re-staged all modified files, requiring an amend to fix the commit message.
A 529 server overload interrupted the session at the final commit/amend step.
open_items: Adapter-layer DnD for TreeView (DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls, pointer/keyboard hit-testing, 500ms hover-expand timer, use_drag/use_drop wiring) is deferred to #366 (Leptos adapter) and #509 (Dioxus adapter).
Lazy Loading variant (spec §5) is out of scope for this task; no implementation provided.
Renamable Nodes variant (spec §6) is out of scope; spec was updated to use Key identity but no implementation provided.

The 6 MISSED + 2 timeout mutants from the first mutation run had killer tests added, but were not re-verified to 0 missed after the policy change stopped the rerun. Nightly CI will surface any survivors.
Pre-existing MAX_TREE_DEPTH intra-doc-link warning in tree_collection.rs struct doc was not fixed (pre-existing noise, not introduced by this task).
83 pre-existing rustdoc warnings across ars-components were not addressed (outside task scope).
